reasoning: The informal statement claims that exactly (p-1)/2 of the nonzero residues mod p are quadratic residues and (p-1)/2 are nonresidues. The Lean statement asserts the existence of a Finset S1 of nonzero QRs with |S1|=(p-1)/2 and a Finset S2 of nonzero non-QRs with |S2|=(p-1)/2. Though these "existence" forms individually look weaker than "exactly", their conjunction is equivalent: S1 and S2 are disjoint subsets of ZMod p \ {0} (size p-1), and (p-1)/2 + (p-1)/2 = p-1 forces both to be exactly the full sets of QRs and non-QRs. Note that {q : ∃x, x²=q} coincides with the squares in ZMod p (and 0 is excluded from S), and "¬∃x, x²=q" automatically excludes 0 since 0 is a square, so the encoding matches the informal claim. With p an odd prime (from Nat.Prime p and p%2=1), the informal and Lean claims are logically equivalent.
